titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/229

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=186 status=live modified=2022-09-01 10:12:45.403489+00 | translation moa=187 status=live modified=2022-09-14 01:23:16.151699+00

div.p:
M . Dei gratia Rex Scot’ . Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiis . Vicecomitibus
⁊ Omnibus fidelibus suis  salutem. Sciant tam posteri quam presentes me dedisse ⁊ Concessisse
inperpetuam elemosinam . deo ⁊ Ecclesie sancte crucis de edeneburc ⁊ Canonicis deo ibi seruientibus
pro salute antecessorum ⁊ successorum meorum ecclesiam de trauernant cum omnibus Iuste ad eam
pertinentibus sicut Walerannus Capellanus unquam melius eam habuit ⁊ tenuit . salua tenura
ipsius Waleranni Capellani quandiu in conuersatione seculari manserit . Volo itaque
ut predicti Canonici ecclesiam illam in perpetuis temporibus post Waleranni dicessum ita libere
⁊ quiete obtineant sicut alias suas habent ⁊ tenent elemosinas ⁊ possessiones . Testibus
Ada Comitissa matre mea . Hugone De Moreuill’ . Waltero Cancellario . Waltero filio alani
Nicolao clerico ipso Waleranno presente ⁊ hoc postulante apud EdeneBVRG

p.cl: Malcolm by the grace of God king of Scots to the Bishops, Abbots, Earls, Barons, Justices, Sheriffs, and all his sworn men, Greeting:

Let those to come as well as those present know that I have given and granted in perpetual alms, to God and the church of Holyrood at Edinburgh and to the canons serving God there, for the salvation of my ancestors and successors, the church of Tranent with everything rightfully belonging to it, just as Waleran the chaplain ever had and held it well, reserving the tenure of Waleran the chaplain himself for as long as he remains in secular life. I therefore will that the aforesaid Canons hold that church in perpetuity after Walter’s departure, as freely and quietly as they have and hold their other alms and possessions.
As witnesses, Countess Ada my mother, Hugh de Morville, Walter the chancellor, Walter son of Alan, Nicholas the clerk, Waleran himself being present and suggesting it.
